commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Contributor-supplied Android proof remains the blocker, and automation cannot provide proof from the contributor's device setup.; Cleared: The diff only changes Android recovery copy selection and local unit tests; it does not alter secrets, dependencies, CI, install scripts, or auth protocol behavior. (sha=6b45ac16aeec43356e8b0473a5bb6cbd4b886ab0)
commands.review_summary:
The branch changes Android onboarding recovery copy to prefer structured GatewayConnectionProblem auth and pairing guidance over generic address/unreachable text, with focused unit tests.

PR surface: Other +130. Total +130 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main can receive structured GatewayConnectionProblem details, but recoveryGatewayDetail selects a nonblank saved address before checking those details. I did not run a live Android current-main reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor-supplied Android proof remains the blocker, and automation cannot provide proof from the contributor's device setup.

target.title: fix(android): clarify gateway auth recovery states
target.body:
Related: #98046

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where Android users recovering gateway setup would see generic gateway-unreachable/authentication messaging when the gateway had already returned a structured auth failure such as an expired setup code, stale saved credential, missing token, or password mismatch.

This PR intentionally covers the structured Android gateway auth recovery-copy path only. Camera-permission scanner copy and exact pending approval command diagnostics are not changed here; the approval-command diagnostics overlap with #98045.

## Why This Change Was Made

The Android recovery detail now consumes the existing `GatewayConnectionProblem` code and recommended next-step fields before falling back to generic copy. Pairing approval handling stays on the existing approval-command path, retryable `PAIRING_REQUIRED` keeps the existing auto-retry guidance, and this change does not add new gateway protocol fields.

## User Impact

Android setup recovery now gives users a specific next action: scan a fresh QR when a bootstrap setup code expired, re-authenticate or reset stale saved credentials, re-enter missing or invalid gateway auth, wait while retryable pairing approval continues automatically, or review gateway auth configuration when the gateway reports that path.

## Evidence

- Claim proved: Android recovery now prefers structured non-ready `GatewayConnectionProblem` guidance over a stale/nonblank saved gateway address, while preserving the address for ready states and generic fallback cases. It also distinguishes existing structured gateway auth detail codes instead of falling through to `Gateway unreachable`, and retryable `PAIRING_REQUIRED` still renders Android pairing/auto-retry guidance on a real device.
- Head under review: `6b45ac16aeec43356e8b0473a5bb6cbd4b886ab0` (`fix(android): prefer auth recovery detail before stale address`).
- Commands / artifacts:
  - `git diff --check` passed locally after the remote-address ordering fix.
  - `apps/android/gradlew.bat :app:testPlayDebugUnitTest --tests "ai.openclaw.app.ui.OnboardingFlowLogicTest" --no-daemon --console=plain --rerun-tasks` passed locally on Windows/Android SDK; Gradle reported `BUILD SUCCESSFUL in 1m 29s` with `30 actionable tasks: 30 executed`.
  - `python .agents\skills\autoreview\scripts\autoreview --mode local` passed after the ordering fix with `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported` and reviewer summary `overall: patch is correct (0.86)`. The helper emitted a late Windows GBK reader-thread decode exception after the clean result; no code finding was reported.
  - GitHub CI on head `6b45ac16aeec43356e8b0473a5bb6cbd4b886ab0` passed the Android lanes:
    - `android-test-play` SUCCESS: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28444432978/job/84290094290
    - `android-test-third-party` SUCCESS: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28444432978/job/84290094209
    - `android-build-play` SUCCESS: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28444432978/job/84290094239
  - PR context/evidence gate passed on the new head: `Real behavior proof` SUCCESS: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28444483166/job/84290171687
  - Real Android proof on Windows host with Android SDK installed:
    - `adb devices -l` showed `3B665800MT900000 device product:PLW110 model:PLW110 device:OP61B7L1 transport_id:2`.
    - `adb shell getprop ro.product.model` -> `PLW110`; `adb shell getprop ro.build.version.release` -> `16`; `adb shell getprop ro.build.version.sdk` -> `36`.
    - Installed this PR branch debug app with `apps/android/gradlew.bat :app:installPlayDebug --no-daemon`; Gradle reported `Installed on 1 device` for `openclaw-2026.6.10-play-debug.apk`.
    - `adb reverse tcp:18789 tcp:18789` established the USB-only tunnel used by the Android app.
    - Captured real-device screenshot locally with `adb shell screencap -p` then `adb pull`: `E:\code\openclaw-android-proof-tools\artifacts\20260630-201308\openclaw-android-proof.png` (not committed to the repo per PR artifact policy).
    - Captured real-device UIAutomator output from the same screen. Relevant visible text excerpt:

```xml
<node text="Gateway Recovery" package="ai.openclaw.app" />
<node text="Pairing Gateway" package="ai.openclaw.app" />
<node text="Approval is in progress.
OpenClaw will reconnect automatically." package="ai.openclaw.app" />
<node text="Home Gateway" package="ai.openclaw.app" />
<node text="Gateway paired. Checking node capability approval." package="ai.openclaw.app" />
<node text="Pairing" package="ai.openclaw.app" />
```

  - The structured pairing proof server was a local WebSocket fixture that sent the normal `connect.challenge` event, then returned a structured connect response with `code: "PAIRING_REQUIRED"`, `reason: "not-paired"`, `recommendedNextStep: "wait_then_retry"`, `retryable: true`, and `pauseReconnect: false`. Raw fixture logs were not pasted because Android connect frames include device tokens/signatures; the public evidence above uses the real-device visible UI text instead.
- Observed result: focused unit coverage includes bootstrap expiry, stale credential/device-token mismatch, password mismatch, missing token, device identity required, recommended-next-step fallback, unknown-message fallback, retryable `PAIRING_REQUIRED` detail copy with status text that does not itself contain pairing/approval wording, and the ClawSweeper-requested case where `remoteAddress` is nonblank while `gatewayConnectionProblem` contains a structured auth failure. The OPPO Reno15 real-device run rendered the pairing recovery page with `Approval is in progress. OpenClaw will reconnect automatically.` from a structured retryable pairing response.
- Not tested / proof gaps: production Gateway setup-code onboarding was also exercised over USB and reached `Node Approval Pending` on the same OPPO device, but the exact retryable pairing-copy screenshot required a local structured WebSocket fixture because current Gateway setup-code bootstrap auto-approves first-time node pairing quickly enough to skip the intermediate `PAIRING_REQUIRED` screen. The PR has focused unit coverage for newly mapped auth-detail strings, but the real-device visible proof captures retryable structured pairing recovery rather than one of the auth-detail strings such as expired setup code or stale credentials.
target.author: qingminglong

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
